water_temp_read.read: Water temperature is in a favorable trout range.

light_pressure_read.read: High pressure and low cloud cover make this fishable but technical.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

caution_notes: Bright/high-pressure conditions may make fish cover-oriented and less forgiving.

generated_text.quick_read: Current trout fishing conditions on the Chattahoochee River are good but technical. Water temperature and recent stocking create opportunity, but bright, high-pressure conditions require careful presentations.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 1 morning due to the combination of ideal water temperature, low wind, low cloud cover, and minimal rain chance that together offer the most comfortable feeding lanes and allow for effective use of smaller natural flies and stocked trout attractors.
generated_text.current_conditions: Current USGS conditions report a water temperature of 59°F, flow of 771 cfs, gage height at 1.83 ft, and turbidity at 2.3 NTU.
generated_text.weather_setup: Weather shows partly cloudy skies around 22% cover, stable high pressure at 30.1 inHg, a light northeast wind averaging 1.6 mph with gusts up to 10.4 mph, and a low 16% chance of rain with no expected accumulation.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout are expected to hold near riffles, runs, seams, structure, and shaded or deeper areas due to high light and pressure. Fish cautiously using nymphs, midges, soft hackles, and opportunistic streamers with clean drifts and smaller natural patterns. Recent stocking supports use of egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracell attractor flies. Anglers should note bright conditions make fish more cover-oriented and less aggressive.
